events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes a LOTO procedure with multiple violations: breaker held with red tape but no lock, paper tag taped to panel door, no verification of zero energy, no mention of stored mechanical energy. This is clearly non-compliant and dangerous. Decision must be fail. Risk is critical. Work stage is in-progress. Component conditions: non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 20. Value 0. Confidence high. Order empty. Workable empty. Findings and actions list. References OSHA 1910.147.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 20,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.98,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Disconnect breaker is switched off and held with red tape only; no authorized lockout device is installed",
    "Tag is a paper tag taped to the panel door; it is not a durable tagout device and is not attached at the isolation point",
    "No individual lock and key applied by each authorized employee; group/personal lockout not satisfied",
    "No verification of zero energy state before servicing; try-step/test-for-absence-of-energy omitted",
    "Stored mechanical energy in the raised conveyor section is not identified, released, or blocked"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and do not begin conveyor servicing until LOTO is corrected",
    "Apply an approved lockout device to the disconnect breaker with an individual lock and key for each authorized employee",
    "Attach a durable tag at the isolation point identifying the worker and date",
    "Release or block stored mechanical energy in the raised conveyor section using blocks, chocks, or rated supports",
    "Verify zero energy by attempting to start the conveyor and testing voltage at the load side of the disconnect",
    "Update the work order checklist to include zero-energy verification and stored-energy control before work continues"
  ],
  "rationale": "Red tape and a paper tag do not constitute energy isolation under OSHA 1910.147. The breaker must be locked out with an approved device, stored mechanical energy must be controlled, and zero energy must be verified before any servicing begins. The current state is non-compliant and presents a critical risk of unexpected energization or gravity release.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(c) - Energy isolation device and lockout/tagout application",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d) - Application of control and sequence of lockout/tagout",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(5) - Verification of isolation",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(6) - Release of stored or residual energy"
  ]
}
